methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $29.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Comand AI is a seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Loïc Mougeolle and Félix Petersen that has raised €11.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparables universe of 10 companies (2 public, 8 private) operating in the same sector at seed and Series A stages, including Scale AI, Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and Tenna Systems.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach here. The median valuation across the five most relevant peers—weighted toward same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies—is $300M, with a range spanning $60M (Tenna Systems, seed stage) to $29.0B (Scale AI, Series A). This wide dispersion reflects the heterogeneity typical of defense-tech, where stage advancement and customer traction drive substantial valuation divergence.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $255M to $345M, centered on a $300M median. The key uncertainties are (1) the limited depth of the comparable set at true seed stage within defense applications, (2) the extreme outlier represented by Scale AI's $29.0B valuation, which may not be directly comparable despite sector alignment, and (3) the absence of revenue or customer concentration data that would typically calibrate stage-appropriate multiples. Additional methods—such as venture capital method or revenue multiples—would strengthen conviction if applicable metrics were available.
